channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: OpenTable names 2024's 'Top 100 Restaurants' in America
item.description: OpenTable's analysts based their findings on more than 14 million diner reviews, along with diner ratings, reservation demand, and percentage of five-star reviews, among other factors.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Jonathan Majors’ ex-girlfriend drops assault, defamation suit
item.description: The civil suit was prompted by the Marvel and “Creed III” actor’s repeated public denial that he attacked Jabbari.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Texas is taking a final vote on allowing Bible-infused lessons in public schools
item.description: Texas education officials will soon decide if a controversial Bible-infused curriculum can be taught in public schools.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Diddy private eyes confront influencers about possible payments
item.description: TikTokers Samson Crouppen and Amala Ekpunobi deny any pay arrangement and expressed fear and concern over the visits.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian police indict Bolsonaro for alleged attempted coup, threatening his political career
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Police indicted Brazil’s former president Jair Bolsonaro and 36 others for allegedly attempting a coup to keep the right-wing leader in office after his defeat in the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators express relief over Gaetz’s decision to withdraw from AG consideration
item.description: Sen. Roger Wicker (R-Miss.), the incoming chair of the Senate Armed Services Committee, welcomed Gaetz’s decision to withdraw as “a positive development.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The top official for North Carolina's efforts to rebuild communities after natural disasters is no longer working in the position just a few days after a scathing rebuke of her work from GOP state lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US towns plunge into debates about fluoride in water
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For about 50 years, adding cavity-preventing fluoride to drinking water was a popular public health measure in Yorktown, a leafy town north of New York City. But in September, the town’s supervisor used his emergency powers to stop the practice.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia's Brian Kemp picked to lead Republican Governors Association
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will lead the Republican Governors Association for the next year, the group announced Wednesday, just weeks after the party secured governorships in eight out of 11 state races up for grabs in the election earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father of Georgia high school shooting suspect pleads not guilty
item.description: Colin Gray, the father of Apalachee High School shooter Colt Gray, has pleaded not guilty to the 29 counts facing him during a court hearing Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: St. Louis was once known as Mound City for its many Native American mounds. Just one remains
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — What is now St. Louis was once home to more than 100 mounds constructed by Native Americans — so many that St. Louis was once known as “Mound City.” Settlers tore most of them down, and just one remains.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: Laken Riley trial highlights Biden-era immigration crisis as mom of slain cheerleader awaits justice
item.description: A Georgia judge has reached a verdict for Laken Riley murder suspect Jose Ibarra. Prosecutors say the Venezuelan national killed Riley while she was jogging on UGA's campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “ radicals, zealots and Marxists.” He has picked Linda McMahon, a former wrestling executive, to lead the department.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecution rests in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Prosecutors finished calling witnesses Tuesday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley, and the defense began presenting its case but quickly ran into snags. Jose Ibarra, 26, is charged with murder and other crimes in Riley’s February death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tracking the Tropics | 2024 hurricane season nears its end
item.description: With just two weeks left to go in the 2024 hurricane season, the tropics appear to have quieted down.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: The White House's Christmas tree is a symbol of resilience for hurricane-hit North Carolina farms
item.description: The Cartner family had known since last year that one of their farm’s Christmas trees would be headed to the White House this winter.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson becomes 'MAGA' Mike, cementing place in Trump's circle
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is cementing his place in President-elect Trump’s inner orbit, becoming not only a visible — if slightly out-of-place-looking — part of his entourage, but a key supporter of his no-holds-barred approach to his second term.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, Fox 5

item.title: 3 people are killed in random stabbings in New York City. A suspect is in custody
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man fatally stabbed three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Agent says GPS data puts Georgia student Laken Riley in same area as man accused in her death
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — An FBI special agent testified Monday that electronic location data seems to place Georgia nursing student Laken Riley and the man accused of killing her in the same wooded area at the time of her death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal appeals court won't block upcoming nitrogen gas execution in Alabama
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — A federal appeals court said Monday it will let the nation’s third execution with nitrogen gas go forward this week in Alabama, rejecting arguments that the new method causes unconstitutional levels of pain. The 11th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plastic bag bans can sometimes backfire: Study
item.description: Policies aimed at curbing the use of single-use plastic bags have sometimes accomplished the opposite of their intentions, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, WGN 9

item.title: Steve Bannon's trial in a border wall charity scheme case delayed until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon 's border wall fundraising trial was postponed to February as prosecutors disclosed Monday that the former Trump White House strategist himself once suggested the nonprofit venture was “a scam.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship in Hawaii
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
